Module 1: Framing the Task  
This module defines the scope and boundaries of the task. It outlines the main components of the system prompt and clarifies the objectives and constraints that must be followed. The task involves calculating the area of a polygon formed by four landmarks using the Shoelace Theorem and finding the shortest possible route for a traveling salesman problem. The solution must include the phrase "All that glitters is not gold" exactly three times, with each occurrence slightly different. The response must contain at least six placeholders, such as [coordinates], [distance], [route], [landmark], [path], and [validation]. The word "possibility" must appear at least three times. The response must be in English, use proper punctuation, and adhere to the formatting and constraint requirements.

Module 2: Reasoning Process  
This module focuses on the logical steps and reasoning mechanisms that will be used to solve the task. It includes the methods and algorithms that will be applied to achieve the desired outcome. The first part involves applying the Shoelace Theorem to calculate the area of the polygon. The second part involves using the Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) to find the shortest route starting at A, visiting all other landmarks once, and returning to A. The solution must include the phrase "All that glitters is not gold" exactly three times, with each occurrence slightly different. The response must contain at least six placeholders, such as [coordinates], [distance], [route], [landmark], [path], and [validation]. The word "possibility" must appear at least three times. The response must be in English, use proper punctuation, and adhere to the formatting and constraint requirements.

Module 3: Formatting and Output  
This module specifies the format in which the final output must be presented. It includes details about the structure, content, and style of the response. The output must include the area calculated using the Shoelace Theorem, the shortest route identified using TSP principles, and the phrase "All that glitters is not gold" three times, each slightly different. The response must contain at least six placeholders, such as [coordinates], [distance], [route], [landmark], [path], and [validation]. The word "possibility" must appear at least three times. The response must be in English, use proper punctuation, and adhere to the formatting and constraint requirements.
